title: DPC Dash Ltd (1405.HK) Announces 2023 Interim Financial Results with 51.5% YoY Revenue Growth
text: Delivered record half year results with total revenue of RMB1.38 Billion, a 51.5% YoY growth Store-level operating profit grew 123.6%; Adjusted Group EBITDA increased 128.6% Added 84 net new stores in the 1H2023; Operating 672 stores across 20 cities, as of June 30 Six newly opened stores in newly entered cities broke Domino's Pizza global First-30-Day-Sales Record, demonstrating strong brand recognition and brand momentum HONG KONG , Aug. 29, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- DPC Dash Ltd ("DPC Dash" or the "Company", together with its subsidiaries, the "Group") (1405.HK), the exclusive master franchisee of Domino's Pizza (NYSE: DPZ) in Mainland China, Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of China and Macau Special Administrative Region of China, today announced its unaudited consolidated interim results for the six months ended June 30, 2023 (the "Interim Results") (the "Announcement"). FIRST HALF OF 2023 HIGHLIGHTS [1] All comparisons were made on a year-over-year ("YoY") basis.

title: 5 Stocks to Add to Your Portfolio on Soaring Restaurant Sales
text: Restaurant sales have been steadily growing which is helping stocks like Yum China Holdings, Inc. (YUMC), McDonald's Corporation (MCD), Carrols Restaurant Group, Inc. (TAST), Domino's Pizza, Inc. (DPZ) and BJ's Restaurants, Inc. (BJRI).

title: Domino's Pizza will close all 142 stores in Russia
text: Domino's Pizza will close all its outlets in Russia, becoming one of the first major Western fast-food chains to exit the country since McDonald's and Starbucks left more than a year ago.

title: Why Didn't We Launch This Sooner?! Domino's® Introduces Pepperoni Stuffed Cheesy Bread
text: Pepperoni lovers can now indulge in the newest type of Stuffed Cheesy Bread ANN ARBOR, Mich., Aug. 21, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Domino's Pizza Inc. (NYSE: DPZ) is giving pepperoni lovers more of what they want.

title: Domino's Pizza: Quality And Speed Competitors Can't Replicate
text: Domino's Pizza is the leading player in the quick-service restaurant (QSR) pizza industry, with a strong market share and global presence. The company's investment in technology and delivery systems has helped it maintain a competitive edge and strong customer loyalty. While the company faces challenges in a competitive market and potential balance sheet risks, its strong brand, consistent growth, and potential for international expansion make it a promising long-term investment.

title: What's Behind Domino's (DPZ) 22% Increase in Three Months?
text: Domino's (DPZ) benefits from strong expansion initiatives, impressive comparable sales growth, adoption of digital technologies and continuous menu innovation.

title: Domino's Pizza: Decelerating Growth Priced To Perfection
text: Domino's Pizza has reported mixed Q2 earnings with EPS rising by 9.2%, but revenue seeing a 3.2% decrease compared to the prior year same quarter. The announcement of the Uber deal, where 70% of the Uber Eats customers can with ease order Domino's pizza, has sparked a stock rally bringing DPZ to fair value territory. The fair valuation coupled with declining revenue and operating income growth over the last 3 years warrants a cautious outlook with unlikely short-term gains.

title: Add 5 Low-Beta Stocks to Your Portfolio to Counter Volatility
text: We have narrowed our search to five low-beta stocks. These are: DPZ, ACGL, AZPN, MLM, ERIE.

title: Domino's® Pizza is 50% Off This Week!

title: Domino's Pizza: Long-Term Prospects Remain Solid
text: DPZ delivered up their latest quarter, and we saw a bit of a mixed picture with EPS topping estimates but revenue coming in light. However, earnings estimates have historically been mixed, and this quarter it's just essentially a continuation of that. I believe the greater potential headwind for the stock price is the significant rise that it has experienced in such a short period which could pose some near-term pressures.

title: Is It Time to Buy Domino's Stock?
text: The pizza chain is highly profitable, but growth has been sluggish. The stock's lower valuation makes sense right now.

title: Domino's Pizza jumps 7% as it readies to serve up a large slice of cash
text: Shares Domino's Pizza Group PLC rose 7% after the company announced a £70 million share buyback programme and an increase in its annual profit forecast. The uptick in orders and sales in the first half of the year has given the company reason to be optimistic, despite ongoing economic uncertainty.
